verses.text: The Lamb stood on Sion with 'an hundred forty and four thousand,' the Father's name on them, singing 'a new song' none else could learn. The Lamb's own. The redeemed sing what only they can sing.
The Lamb stood on Sion with 'an hundred forty and four thousand,' the Father's name on them, singing 'a new song' none else could learn. The Lamb's own. The redeemed sing what only they can sing.
The Lamb stood on Sion with 'an hundred forty and four thousand,' the Father's name on them, singing 'a new song' none else could learn. The Lamb's own. The redeemed sing what only they can sing.
These follow the Lamb 'whithersoever he goeth,' firstfruits to God, 'without fault before the throne.' Faultless. The Lamb's followers are pure and wholly His.
These follow the Lamb 'whithersoever he goeth,' firstfruits to God, 'without fault before the throne.' Faultless. The Lamb's followers are pure and wholly His.
An angel flies with 'the everlasting gospel,' crying, 'Fear God... worship him that made heaven and earth.' Gospel proclaimed. The call to worship the Creator goes to all.
An angel flies with 'the everlasting gospel,' crying, 'Fear God... worship him that made heaven and earth.' Gospel proclaimed. The call to worship the Creator goes to all.
Another follows: 'Babylon is fallen, is fallen, that great city,' which made nations drink her wine. Babylon falls. The proud world-city is doomed.
A third warns: whoever worships the beast and takes his mark 'shall drink of the wine of the wrath of God,' tormented for ever. Dread warning. The beast's worshippers face unending judgment.
A third warns: whoever worships the beast and takes his mark 'shall drink of the wine of the wrath of God,' tormented for ever. Dread warning. The beast's worshippers face unending judgment.
A third warns: whoever worships the beast and takes his mark 'shall drink of the wine of the wrath of God,' tormented for ever. Dread warning. The beast's worshippers face unending judgment.
'Here is the patience of the saints'; 'Blessed are the dead which die in the Lord... they rest from their labours.' Blessed rest. The faithful dead are at peace, their works following.
'Here is the patience of the saints'; 'Blessed are the dead which die in the Lord... they rest from their labours.' Blessed rest. The faithful dead are at peace, their works following.
One 'like unto the Son of man' on a cloud with a sickle reaps the earth, 'for the harvest of the earth is ripe.' Harvest. The Lord gathers His ripened field.
One 'like unto the Son of man' on a cloud with a sickle reaps the earth, 'for the harvest of the earth is ripe.' Harvest. The Lord gathers His ripened field.
One 'like unto the Son of man' on a cloud with a sickle reaps the earth, 'for the harvest of the earth is ripe.' Harvest. The Lord gathers His ripened field.
Another angel gathers the vine of the earth into 'the great winepress of the wrath of God,' which is trodden, and blood flows. Winepress. The wicked are crushed in God's judgment.
